en: There's no explanation for it. But Hitler survived, but he was quite injured, which Nazi propaganda always denied.

en: Like they always said, the Führer was miraculously unharmed, but he was quite harmed. There were like over a hundred splinters from the wood everywhere.

en: His eardrums were blown, which was, you know, it's quite an injury, I guess, you know, he was bleeding internally and he was shell-shocked basically.

en: And then a new doctor comes in, his name is Giesing, because Morell was not a, in Germany we have, well, I guess it's worldwide.
ko: 그건 꽤 큰 부상이지. 내부 출혈도 있었고, 기본적으로 충격에 빠졌어. 그리고 새로운 의사가 들어왔어. 그의 이름은 기징이야. 독일에는, 전 세계적으로도 그렇겠지만, 이비인후과 전문의가 있어. 독일 군대의 이비인후과 전문의인 기징 박사가 폭탄 공격 후 히틀러의 터진 고막을 치료하러 호출되었어.

en: It's the ear, nose and throat specialist, right?
ko: 그건 이비인후과 전문의야, 맞지?

en: So an ear, nose and throat specialist from the German army called Dr. Giesing, he was ordered to come into headquarters after the bomb attack to treat Hitler's blown ear drums.
ko: 기징은 히틀러에게 코카인을 줬어. 그 당시 코카인은 사용되고 있었거든. 하지만 히틀러는 코카인을 한 번도 복용해본 적이 없었고, 매우 흥미로워했어.

en: And Giesing gave Hitler cocaine, because cocaine at the time was being, was, you know, it was used, it was not schedule one, you know, it was, it had the effect that it would numb the pain and you could, you could like use it, you would like put it on a certain place where you had the pain and then it would numb that area.

en: So Giesing was trying to get Morell out, maybe because he wanted Hitler to have a better health.
ko: 히믈러는 이미 모렐이 히틀러와 너무 많은 시간을 보내는 걸 의심하고 있었어. 통제가 없으니까. 히믈러는 통제광이었어.

en: Maybe he wanted to have the job himself. He certainly tried to get rid of Morell.
ko: 아마도 그는 그 일을 스스로 하고 싶었을 거야. 확실히 모렐을 없애려고 했어.

en: And it came to like the high noon situation, like the duel between the two doctors.

en: He invents history and he's very open about it. This is not what actually happened.
ko: 쉽지 않은 일이야. 쉽지 않은 일이야. 약물에 대한 영화는 타란티노 스타일로 가기 쉽거든.

en: I think a blitz movie would have to stick to the facts. And I've spoken with some directors, very good German directors, and it's just very hard to do.
ko: 약물에 대한 영화는 타란티노 스타일로 가기 쉽거든. 그건 정말 대단하고 멋지긴 한데, 다른 거야.

en: But if you do it well, that's a legendary movie.
ko: 그는 역사를 창조하고, 그걸 아주 솔직하게 말해.
